steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes a problem (mosquitoes develop resistance to repellents that activate multiple receptors) and a solution (EBF works by activating just one receptor — Or31 — present in all disease-carrying species). "Therefore" signals that the blank must logically extend this single-receptor discovery into a research direction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'identify molecular components similar to EBF that target the activation of Or31 receptors': directly follows from the discovery. If Or31 is the key single receptor, finding more molecules that target it expands the toolkit using the same proven strategy. ✓
  • Choice B — 'investigate alternative methods for extracting EBF molecules from chrysanthemums': focuses on extracting EBF, not on developing new repellents. The passage is about a research direction for new repellents, not manufacturing EBF more efficiently. ✗
  • Choice C — 'verify the precise locations of Or31 and other odor receptors on mosquitoes' antennae': mapping receptor locations is not the next logical step after identifying Or31 as the key target. The passage does not suggest location is the obstacle. ✗
  • Choice D — 'determine the maximum number of different odor receptors that can be activated by a single molecule': this pushes toward activating more receptors, which is the opposite of the passage's insight that single-receptor activation avoids resistance. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
